Marriage and Dowry Offences

Overview

Handling delicate criminal matters related to marriage, including dowry harassment allegations (Section 498-A IPC) and Dowry Prohibition Act violations, with standard trials, anticipatory bail, and quashing proceedings.

Key Areas of Focus

  • ✓ Anticipatory & Regular Bail in 498A IPC
  • ✓ Quashing of FIRs under Section 482 CrPC
  • ✓ Trial Defense & Cross-examination
  • ✓ Mediation & Settlement Assistance

Primary Legislation & Statutes

Indian Penal Code, 1860 (Section 498-A) Dowry Prohibition Act, 1961 Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973

Frequently Asked Questions

If a false 498-A FIR is filed, the accused can apply for anticipatory bail to prevent arrest. They can also file a petition under Section 482 of the CrPC in the High Court for quashing the FIR if there is no prima facie case or if it is malicious.

Need Legal Assistance?

Schedule a confidential consultation with our legal experts today.
